coreScope.name: Core GS Modernization
coreScope.plainEnglishSummary: Modernize the Game Server without breaking existing integrations, then move toward a safer service-based architecture with staged rollout and rollback.
coreScope.whatThisMeans: Many planned deliverables were completed, but the system is still not approved for production cutover because runtime canary checks and security dependency audit work are still incomplete.

parallelWorkstreams.name: New Games / Plinko Workstream
parallelWorkstreams.status: Substantial progress (M1-M4 complete in audited evidence, M5 still planned)
parallelWorkstreams.plainEnglishSummary: A separate new-game delivery track (Plinko) progressed strongly and produced real runtime/performance proofs, but it is not the same thing as core GS cutover readiness.

parallelWorkstreams.name: Casino Manager Workstream
parallelWorkstreams.status: Separate charter and planning artifacts present
parallelWorkstreams.plainEnglishSummary: This is a separate project track with its own charter, schema mapping, and rollout planning. It should not be counted as GS modernization cutover completion.

scopeCreepExamples.title: Phase 8 discrepancy viewer/tooling expansion
scopeCreepExamples.plainEnglishSummary: Useful operator tooling was added around precision discrepancy analysis, but this went beyond the minimum requirement of proving safe 0.001 support.

scopeCreepExamples.title: Phase 9 rename-wave execution governance tooling
scopeCreepExamples.plainEnglishSummary: A detailed governance/process toolset was built for rename waves. This improves safety, but it is broader than a minimal brand-name replacement implementation.

currentCutoverBlockers.title: Protocol adapter runtime canary validation is still failing
currentCutoverBlockers.plainEnglishSummary: The protocol adapter path is running, but the key wallet shadow check still fails, so the team cannot approve cutover.

currentCutoverBlockers.title: Core extracted service canary routing is not approved
currentCutoverBlockers.plainEnglishSummary: Gameplay, wallet, bonus, and history services are up, but their canary routing checks fail, so production ownership cannot move to them yet.

currentCutoverBlockers.title: Security dependency audit and lockfiles are still pending
currentCutoverBlockers.plainEnglishSummary: The refactor services still need dependency lockfiles and a proper audit/remediation pass before a production cutover decision.

latestEvidenceSources.label: Latest Program Readiness Report (factual cutover status)

whatNeedsApprovalNext: Approve a focused runtime canary activation/validation wave for Phase 4 protocol adapter routing.
Approve a focused runtime canary activation/validation wave for Phase 5 core extracted services (gameplay/wallet/bonus/history).
Approve a dependency lockfile + audit remediation pass in a network-capable environment.
Keep Casino Manager and New Games reporting separate from core GS cutover status to avoid progress confusion.
plainEnglishSummary: This dashboard separates delivery output progress from production cutover readiness. The core modernization delivered many artifacts, but the project is still blocked on runtime canary validation and security audit work. Parallel workstreams (New Games and Casino Manager) are real work, but they should not be counted as core cutover completion.
